visa_free.nationality: British nationals (Overseas), British overseas territories citizens, British overseas citizens, British protected persons, British subjects

eta.nationality: All Annex II visa-free nationalities (see visa_free list) — ETIAS pending
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorization System) is expected to have a soft launch in Q4 2026. Once active, nationals of Annex II visa-free countries will need prior travel authorization (ETA-equivalent) before entering the Schengen area. Mandatory enforcement is not expected until at least April 2027 after a 6-month airline transition period. Not yet operational as of June 2026. Source: travel-europe.europa.eu revised timeline and visahq.com news January 2026.

visa_policy.notes: Spain is a Schengen member state; visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806 (consolidated version as of 2025-12-30). Annex II lists visa-free third countries (90 days/180-day period). EU/EEA/Swiss citizens have freedom of movement with no visa or stay limits. Nationals of countries in Annex I of the regulation require a Schengen visa. Notable recent changes: Vanuatu was removed from Annex II (effective 14 January 2025, Regulation EU 2025/11); Albania added in 2010 (biometric passports only). ETIAS electronic travel authorization soft launch expected Q4 2026; mandatory enforcement not before April 2027.

credential.subtype: any valid residence permit issued by a Schengen State

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit issued by any Schengen State may move freely within other Schengen States (including Spain) for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without requiring a separate Schengen visa, provided they hold a valid travel document. Applies to nationals who would otherwise require a visa. Residence permit stay periods are not counted against the 90-day visa-free allowance. Authority: Article 21 of the Convention Implementing the Schengen Agreement; confirmed on Spain's official consular entry conditions page.

credential.subtype: valid long-stay visa (Type D national visa) issued by a Schengen State

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen State may move freely within other Schengen States (including Spain) for up to 90 days in any 180-day period without requiring a separate Schengen visa. Applies to nationals who would otherwise require a visa. Confirmed on Spain's official consular entry conditions page: 'This visa will not be necessary if the interested party has a valid residence permit or a valid long-term visa issued by a Schengen State.'

Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Algeria are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) — Information on national derogations from the visa requirement under Regulation (EU) 2018/1806.

Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023). Note: ordinary Indian passport holders require a Schengen visa.

Holders of diplomatic, service and special passports issued by Oman are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement signed 5 November 2025, entered into force 7 January 2026 per Royal Decree 3/2026 (Oman Official Gazette). Confirmed by Spain's Ministry of Foreign Affairs on 5 January 2026. Also recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) for an earlier agreement covering diplomatic and service passports.

Holders of Qatari diplomatic and special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Spain for stays not exceeding 90 days within any 180-day period, provided no remunerated activities are undertaken. 'Acuerdo sobre supresión de los requisitos de visado para los titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos, especiales y de servicio entre el Reino de España y el Estado de Qatar, hecho en Madrid y Doha el 26 de octubre de 2020.' Entry into force: 12 November 2021 (BOE-A-2021-17459). Note: The agreement covers Spain's diplomatic and service passports and Qatar's diplomatic and special passports.

Holders of Saudi Arabian diplomatic, service and special passports are exempt from visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement signed 15 May 2026. Not yet recorded in an EU OJ update at time of research. Official confirmation from Spain's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

Holders of diplomatic and service/official passports issued by Tanzania are exempt from Schengen visa requirements for Spain. Bilateral agreement recorded in EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023).

Holders of diplomatic passports issued by Uzbekistan are exempt from visa requirements for Spain. Spain's Council of Ministers authorized signing of reciprocal exemption agreement in February 2025. Agreement details to be published in BOE upon ratification.

visa_free.name: EU / EEA member states (all 27 EU + Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway) + Switzerland

eta.name: United Kingdom (ETA required since April 2025, mandatory enforcement February 2026)
United States (ESTA required under Visa Waiver Program)

outbound_official.notes: Spain's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores, Unión Europea y Cooperación) publishes outbound travel guidance for Spanish citizens through per-country 'Recomendaciones de viaje' (Travel Recommendations) pages at exteriores.gob.es. There is no single consolidated aggregate visa-free list equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin; visa/entry requirements for Spanish citizens are embedded within individual country advisories. The visa_free list above is derived from EU freedom-of-movement rights (EU/EEA/CH) plus the destinations confirmed visa-free on individual MFA country pages and per the inbound EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II reciprocity (confirmed on official per-country pages, e.g. Japan page explicitly states 'No es necesario visado para estancias de hasta 90 días'). Several nominally 'visa-free' destinations require electronic travel authorisation (ETA/ESTA) prior to travel — these are listed in the eta array. The UK ETA became mandatory from February 25 2026 per the Spain London Consulate official advisory. Russian e-visa is available since August 2023 per the Russia country recommendation page. As of June 2026, ETIAS (Schengen ETA for visa-free third-country nationals entering Spain) remains pending launch.

cbi.notes: Spain does not offer a citizenship-by-investment program. Spanish citizenship is obtained by naturalization after 10 years of legal residence (2 years for nationals of Latin American countries, Philippines, Andorra, Equatorial Guinea, Portugal, or Sephardic Jews). The investor residence visa ('Golden Visa') under Law 14/2013 was abolished on April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025. No CBI route exists in Spanish law. Source: https://sede.mjusticia.gob.es/en/tramites/nacionalidad-espanola and https://www.exteriores.gob.es/Consulados/nuevayork/en/ServiciosConsulares/Paginas/Consular/Visado-de-inversor.aspx

programs.name: Investor Residence Visa / Golden Visa (ABOLISHED)
programs.notes: ABOLISHED on April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025 (published BOE January 3, 2025), which eliminated articles 63-67 of Law 14/2013. Investment options were: real estate ≥€500,000 (unencumbered); shares/stocks in Spanish companies ≥€1,000,000; Spanish public debt ≥€2,000,000; business projects of general interest. Applications submitted before April 3, 2025 may still be processed under prior rules. Min_amount reflects the former real estate option (lowest threshold). Citizenship path is via standard naturalization (10 years residence generally).

programs.name: Non-Lucrative Residence Visa
programs.notes: For non-EU nationals who wish to reside in Spain without engaging in work activities. Requires proof of sufficient economic means (400% of monthly IPREM for the applicant, plus 100% per dependent). Initial visa valid 1 year; can be renewed. After 5 years continuous legal residence may apply for long-term residence permit (permanent residence). Path to citizenship is 10 years (2 years for certain nationalities). No minimum investment amount required — based on income/savings proof.

programs.name: Telework Visa (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals working remotely for companies located outside Spain. Must hold a university degree from a prestigious institution or have 3+ years of professional experience. Employees must work exclusively for non-Spanish companies; self-employed may work up to 20% for Spanish entities.
programs.notes: Established under Law 28/2022 (Startup Law) amending Law 14/2013. Visa valid up to 1 year; can be converted to residence authorization (up to 3 years) via UGE-CE. Income threshold: 200% of monthly SMI (€2,442/month as of 2026). Processed by UGE-CE (Unidad de Grandes Empresas y Colectivos Estratégicos).

programs.name: Entrepreneur Visa (Startup/Innovative Project)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals planning to launch an innovative business project of particular economic interest for Spain. Requires a favourable report from the Economic and Commercial Office evaluating job creation, qualifications, and economic value.
programs.notes: Established under Law 14/2013 as amended by Startup Law 28/2022. Financial requirement: 100% of IPREM plus 50% per dependent. Visa valid 1 year. After arrival, can obtain entrepreneur residence authorization through UGE-CE. Covered by Spain's 'Entrepreneurs Law'.

programs.name: Highly Qualified Worker Visa / EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a valid employment contract for highly qualified work. Must hold a university/college degree (3+ years) or 5+ years of professional experience. Minimum gross annual salary €38,844 (2024); reduced threshold €31,075 for shortage occupations or recent graduates.
programs.notes: Two routes: Spain's national Highly Qualified Worker visa (Law 14/2013) and the EU Blue Card. EU Blue Card valid 3 years, renewable for 2 years. EU Blue Card fee: €73.26 initial application, €16.08 card issuance. Processed through UGE-CE at inclusion.gob.es. Visa initially valid 1 year pending residence authorization.

programs.name: Intra-Company Transfer Visa
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals transferring within the same company or group of companies to Spain as senior management, specialists, or trainees.
programs.processing_time: 10 days from submission (visa); 20 working days for residence authorization
programs.notes: Established under Law 14/2013. Visa valid 1 year or duration of residence permit if shorter. Requires Initial Residence Authorization from Spain's Dirección General de Migraciones. Family members may apply concurrently.

agreements: EU freedom of movement (EU citizens and EEA/Swiss equivalents)
Schengen Area (free movement within Schengen zone)

validation_notes: CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Added Albania (ALB) to visa_free list — it was erroneously omitted. Albania has been in EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II since 2010 (biometric passports only, 90 days/180 days). Verified against consolidated regulation text (2025-12-30).

CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Updated ETIAS eta note from 'expected late 2026' to reflect revised timeline: soft launch Q4 2026, mandatory enforcement not before April 2027 per EU official revised timeline and news reports January 2026.
CORRECTION (June 2026 validation): Updated visa_policy.notes to mention Vanuatu removal (effective 14 Jan 2025, Reg. EU 2025/11) and Albania addition, providing context for Annex II changes.
CONFIRMED: Morocco is correctly absent from visa_free list (removed from Annex II; remains in conditional_access for diplomatic/service/special passports only).
CONFIRMED: Vanuatu is correctly absent from visa_free list (removed effective 14 January 2025 by Regulation EU 2025/11).
CONFIRMED: Spain's Golden Visa (investor residence) abolished April 3, 2025 by Organic Law 1/2025. Record correctly reflects this.
CONFIRMED: Digital nomad visa income threshold of €2,442/month is correct for 2026 (200% of 2026 SMI of €1,221/month, per BOE publication February 2026).
CONFIRMED: EU Regulation 2018/1806 consolidated version date 2025-12-30 is correct and most recent as of validation date.
gaps: Annex I of EU Regulation 2018/1806 (visa-required countries) was not fully enumerated — the official PDF was binary-encoded and unreadable via WebFetch; however, Spain's official consular pages explicitly reference Annex I for visa-required nationals and Annex II for visa-free nationals.
Exact path-to-permanent-residence years for Non-Lucrative Visa not confirmed from official page (5-year standard EU long-term residence rule applied).
EU Blue Card salary threshold sourced from EC migration portal (official EU source, not Spain MFA directly); 2025 figure €38,844 cited — 2026 update not confirmed.
ETIAS soft launch Q4 2026 — exact date not yet announced. Mandatory enforcement expected April 2027 at earliest; not yet operational as of June 2026.
No citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program exists for Spain — confirmed from multiple official sources.
Spain's Golden Visa (investor residence) was abolished April 3, 2025 — some consular pages may not yet be fully updated to reflect this.

No official source found for Spain conditional entry based on holding a US visa (B1/B2 or other category) or US Green Card alone — aggregator sites (non-official) suggest no such exemption exists for Spain; confirmed: holding a US visa does NOT grant visa-free access to Spain for nationals who otherwise require a Schengen visa.
India OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no Spain-specific official rule found granting entry to Spain based on OCI status. OCI confers rights in India, not Spain. No entry added.

Spain-Saudi Arabia diplomatic/service passport agreement alleged 'signed 15 May 2026' — no BOE publication reference found; source_url set to exteriores.gob.es domain only. Status unverified; retain with caution pending BOE publication.
Spain-Uzbekistan diplomatic passport agreement (February 2025): Council of Ministers authorized signing; awaiting BOE publication. Exact entry into force date not confirmed.

EU OJ 2023/C 340/09 (27 September 2023) is the most recent available consolidated list of Spain's bilateral diplomatic/service passport waivers; any agreements concluded after that date (Oman 2026, Saudi Arabia 2026, Uzbekistan 2025) may not yet appear in an EU OJ update.

Complete list of countries with bilateral agreements for diplomatic passport waivers with Spain may have been updated since the September 2023 EU OJ publication. Peru (BOE-A-2000-23920) and Ecuador (BOE-A-2004-2217) have older BOE-confirmed agreements.

Georgia: EU home-affairs portal noted a 'suspension of visa-free travel for diplomatic, service, and official passport holders effective March 6, 2026' — if confirmed by Spain, the Georgia diplomatic/service passport conditional_access entry may need removal. Could not verify via official Spain MFA page in this validation pass; flagged for follow-up.
